chapters.title: Eligibility & Enrollment
chapters.description: Who qualifies for benefits and how to enroll during active open enrollment.
contentParagraphs: If you are a full-time employee of LEHR who is regularly scheduled to work at least 30 hours a week, you are eligible for benefits.
The elections you make are effective 1st of the month following 60 days of employment.
This is an ACTIVE enrollment! Current benefit elections will not roll over. All eligible employees must make elections in order to receive benefits in 2025.

sections.title: Enrollment Requirements
paragraphs: Full-time employees regularly scheduled to work at least 30 hours per week are eligible.
Coverage is effective the 1st of the month following 60 days of employment.
This is an active enrollment — current elections do not roll over. All eligible employees must make new elections to receive benefits in 2025.

chapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
chapters.description: A summary of all benefit options available to Defender Supply employees in 2025.
contentParagraphs: Defender Supply offers a comprehensive benefits package for 2025 covering medical, dental, vision, life and AD&D insurance, short- and long-term disability, and a Health Savings Account.
Medical coverage is provided by UHC and includes three plan options: HDHP, Base Copay, and Buy-Up Copay, all available nationwide.
Voluntary dental and vision coverage is also available through UHC.
Life, AD&D, and disability coverage is provided through Unum. Basic Life and AD&D insurance of $10,000 is provided automatically to all full-time employees.
Employees enrolled in the HDHP may contribute to a Health Savings Account (HSA) through Optum.

This brochure is intended to describe the eligibility requirements, enrollment procedures and coverage effective dates for the benefits offered by the Company. It is not a legal Plan document. Full details are contained in the Summary Plan Descriptions (SPDs).

chapters.title: Medical Benefits
chapters.description: UHC medical plans available nationwide including HDHP, Base Copay, and Buy-Up Copay options.
contentParagraphs: Our medical coverage helps maintain your wellbeing through preventive care, access to an extensive network of providers, and affordable prescription medication.
Medical coverage is provided by UHC. To see a current list of network providers online, visit www.myuhc.com.
UHC medical plans provide coverage for in-network providers only. Out-of-network providers are not covered except for Emergency Room when true emergency.

chapters.title: Voluntary Dental Benefits
chapters.description: Affordable DPPO dental coverage through UHC for routine and major dental care.
contentParagraphs: LEHR offers affordable dental plan options for routine care and beyond. Coverage is available from UHC.
Employees may purchase dental insurance on a voluntary basis.
*After deductible

rows.label: Preventive Services (Oral Exams, Routine Cleanings, Fluoride Applications, Sealants, Space Maintainers)
Basic Services (Fillings, Oral Surgery, Simple Extractions)
Major Services (Denture Adjustments and Repairs, Root Canal Therapy, Periodontics, Crowns, Dentures, Bridges)

chapters.title: Voluntary Vision Benefits
chapters.description: Comprehensive vision coverage through UHC with copays for exams, lenses, contacts, and frames.
contentParagraphs: LEHR offers a comprehensive vision benefit provided by UHC.
Employees may purchase vision insurance on a voluntary basis.
*Fitting and Evaluation fee applied to contact lens allowance.

rows.label: CONTACTS (In Lieu of Lenses and Frames)

chapters.title: Health Savings Account
chapters.description: Tax-free savings account for qualified medical expenses, available to employees enrolled in the HDHP.
contentParagraphs: With a Health Savings Account (HSA), contributions and withdrawals for qualified medical expenses are tax-free.
Your HSA can be used for qualified expenses for you, your spouse and/or tax dependent(s), even if they are not covered by your plan.
If you are not enrolled in a HDHP but you have unused HSA funds from a previous account, those funds can still be used for qualified medical expenses.
The annual maximum contribution limit to HSAs is set by the IRS each year.
Must be enrolled in the HDHP plan to contribute to a Health Savings Account (HSA).
HSA is administered through Optum. Contact: 866-633-2446 | www.myuhc.com

chapters.title: Survivor Benefits
chapters.description: Basic and voluntary life and AD&D insurance through Unum to protect your family financially.
contentParagraphs: LEHR provides employees with Basic Life and AD&D insurance as part of your basic coverage through Unum, which guarantees that your spouse or other designated survivor(s) continue to receive benefits after death.
Your Basic Life and AD&D insurance benefit is $10,000. If you are a full-time employee, you automatically receive Life and AD&D insurance even if you waive other coverage.
You may purchase Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance for yourself in $10,000 increments, up to a maximum of lesser of 5x earnings or $500,000. EOI is required over $100,000.
Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance is available in $5,000 increments for your spouse, up to lesser of employee amount or $250,000. EOI is required over $25,000.
You may purchase Voluntary Life/AD&D insurance in $2,000 increments for your child(ren), up to $10,000. EOI is not required.
Note: Benefits subject to age reduction schedule — 65% at age 65, 50% at age 70.
Voluntary AD&D Insurance: $0.020 per $1,000 coverage (monthly).
Voluntary Child Life Insurance: $0.400 per $1,000 coverage (monthly).

chapters.title: Income Protection
chapters.description: Voluntary short-term and long-term disability coverage through Unum to protect your income during illness or injury.
contentParagraphs: LEHR offers disability coverage to protect you financially in the event you cannot work due to a debilitating illness or injury.
A portion of your income is protected until you can return to work or you reach retirement age.
Employees may purchase disability insurance on a voluntary basis.
Voluntary STD: 60% of your income, up to $1,500/week. 7-day elimination period. Maximum benefit period of 12 weeks.
Voluntary LTD: 60% of your income, up to $6,500/month. 90-day elimination period. Payments last until you are no longer disabled or reach Social Security Normal Retirement Age, whichever is sooner.

rows.label: Maximum Benefit Period
cells: Until disabled or Social Security Normal Retirement Age, whichever is sooner
